'''Aldraig IV''' was a [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located on the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]]. Its surface featured [[Forest/Legends|forests]] and a picturesque countryside. During the [[Alsakan Conflicts/Legends|Alsakan Conflicts]], the planet was affiliated with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world, [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Tens of [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] later, Aldraig IV was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Youngling/Legends|child]] prodigy [[Artist/Legends|artist]] [[Gayla Riemann]], who eventually traveled off-world. During her absence, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] initialized an [[Factory world/Legends|industrialization]] process on the planet, establishing a [[Aldraig IV AT-AT production facility|production facility]] for [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] [[Walker/Legends|walkers]] as well as a [[Aldraig IV TIE fighter hull assembly facility|TIE fighter hull assembly plant]] that endangered the population of a local [[Unidentified Aldraig IV bird|bird]]. When she returned to Aldraig IV, Riemann led protest activities and eventually became involved with a local anti-Imperial [[Aldraig underground|underground resistance movement]], helping it severely damage the [[TIE series/Legends|TIE fighter]] [[Factory/Legends|plant]]. Afterward, Riemann was smuggled off-planet and joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].

==Description==
Aldraig IV, also referred to simply as Aldraig, was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that occupied the fourth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="Truce" /> in the [[Aldraig system/Legends|Aldraig system]]. It was a part of the [[Arrowhead]], or [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. By [[3705 BBY/Legends|3705 BBY]], Aldraig IV was situated between the [[Demophon system/Legends|Demophon]] and [[Skako system/Legends|Skako systems]] on the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace route]] known as the [[Brentaal–Denon Route]]. At some point following [[3694 BBY]], the hyperlane was integrated into the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-spanning [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]].<ref name="TEA" /> Aldraig IV's surface featured large expanses of pristine [[Forest/Legends|forests]],<ref name="CSWE" /> and there were picturesque landscapes in the planet's countryside. The world's southern polar region was home to a rare [[Unidentified Aldraig IV bird|species]] of [[Bird/Legends|bird]].<ref name="Truce" />

==History==
===A world despoiled===
[[File:TheSpinCloseupAlsakanConflicts-EGTW.jpg|thumb|left|300px|During the conflicts between the Galactic Republic worlds Coruscant and Alsakan, Aldraig IV was allied with the former.]]
Aldraig IV fell within the borders of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as early as [[17,000 BBY]]. During the early [[Alsakan Conflicts/Legends|Alsakan Conflicts]], a series of wars that began in [[17,018 BBY]] and saw the Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the planet [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]], and their respective allies struggle over [[Politics/Legends|political]] and economic control over the Republic, Aldraig IV was affiliated with Coruscant.<ref name="EGTW" />

At some point, a bird inhabiting Aldraig IV's southern polar region was [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] nearly to extinction. Subsequently, during the [[Youngling/Legends|childhood]] of the local [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gayla Riemann]], a concerted preservation effort was made that resulted in the [[Species/Legends|species]] repopulating the region. During her time on her [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], Riemann also enjoyed [[Painting/Legends|painting]] the landscapes of its countryside. Eventually, she—being considered a child prodigy and an [[Artist/Legends|artist]] of interplanetary renown—moved offworld into the wider Core Worlds.<ref name="Truce" />

By [[6 BBY/Legends|6 BBY]],<ref name="6 BBY">{{WEGCite|story=Akin from Afar|book=The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook}}, which is set during the [[Bakura Incident]], establishes that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] established an industrial presence on Aldraig IV over the course of at least a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the Bakura Incident to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], meaning that the Imperial activities on Aldraig IV began at some point by [[6 BBY/Legends|6 BBY]].</ref> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] began the process of [[Factory world/Legends|industrializing]] Aldraig IV. Many of the planet's counties were "restructured" from [[Farm/Legends|agricultural]] production to the manufacturing of [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]].<ref name="Truce" /> Large tracts of [[Tree/Legends|woodland]]<ref name="CSWE" /> over the territory of several counties<ref name="Truce" /> were cleared<ref name="CSWE" /> in order to establish a [[Aldraig IV AT-AT production facility|facility]] for producing Imperial<ref name="Truce" /> [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transport]] (AT-AT) [[Walker/Legends|walkers]].<ref name="CSWE AT-AT">{{CSWECite|I|22|All Terrain Armored Transport (AT-AT walker)}}</ref> In addition, in Aldraig IV's south polar area [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] constructed a [[Aldraig IV TIE fighter hull assembly facility|plant]] for assembling [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] [[Hull/Legends|hulls]], with that activity threatening the survival of the population of the local birds. Over time, an [[Aldraig underground|underground resistance movement]] supported by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] began operating on the planet.<ref name="Truce" />

===Resistance===
[[File:GaylaRiemann.png|thumb|right|300px|Gayla Riemann was involved with the anti-Imperial resistance on her native Aldraig IV.]]
Eventually tiring of the elitist artistic community of the rest of the Core Worlds, Riemann returned to Aldraig IV, where she longed to paint the local landscapes again. Upon discovering the Imperial activities there, she staged a peaceful, well-documented protest against the construction of the Sienar Fleet Systems' [[Factory/Legends|facility]]. The local Imperial [[Governor/Legends|governor]] [[Talloryn]], however, had the protesters, including Riemann, arrested and the relevant news reports silenced. After her release from incarceration, Riemann began covertly helping the Aldraig resistance. The underground movement sabotaged the Sienar facility, although the Empire later learned of Riemann's involvement with their activities.<ref name="Truce" />

As part of a "probation" process, Talloryn ordered Riemann to paint portraits of the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] stationed on Aldraig IV as well as create a series of paintings of the TIE plant. Unbeknownst to the governor, however, Riemann used the latter work as cover to develop a floor plan of the facility that she subsequently delivered to the resistance. The Aldraig underground then proceeded to destroy its [[Power core/Legends|power core]] and production floor, after which the artist was smuggled off-planet and, not long before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and the [[Bakura Incident]]<ref name="Truce" /> of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> joined the Rebel Alliance.<ref name="Truce" /> During the latter campaign, Riemann mused that, like her, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of<ref name="Akin">{{WEGCite|story=Akin from Afar|book=The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook}}</ref> the Core Worlds planet [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]<ref name="NEC" /> had also lost her beautiful homeworld to the Empire—although in Organa's case, it had happened in the span of a moment rather than a decade,<ref name="Akin" /> with the Empire having [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroyed]] Alderaan with the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Death Star/Legends|battle station's]] [[Concave Dish Composite Beam Superlaser|superlaser]] [[0 BBY/Legends|four years]] prior.<ref name="NEC" />

==Inhabitants==
At least a part of Aldraig IV was administratively divided into counties, at least several of which were devoted to agricultural production. At some point, the world's inhabitants hunted a local species of bird. The planet was the homeworld of the [[Gender/Legends|female]] Human Gayla Riemann. During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the Galactic Empire, a number of Imperial [[Officer/Legends|officers]] were stationed on the planet, and a local underground resistance movement also operated there.<ref name="Truce" />

==Locations==
The Galactic Empire established an All Terrain Armored Transport production facility on Aldraig IV as well as a TIE fighter hull assembly facility in its southern polar region.<ref name="Truce" />

==Behind the scenes==
Aldraig IV was first mentioned in [[Kathy Tyers]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann]]'s [[1996]] ''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'' of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', including in the short companion narrative "[[Akin from Afar]]."<ref name="Truce" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Aldraig [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Aldraig IV, in grid square M-10.<ref name="TEA" />
